title: Green Protein Smoothie
blurb: The smoothie I make every single morning. Takes 5 minutes, keeps me full until lunch, and tastes better than anything I've bought.
benefits: High in plant protein — keeps you full for hours
Spinach adds iron without any detectable taste
Natural energy boost without the crash

steps.text: Add all ingredients to the blender in this order: liquid first, then soft ingredients, frozen items last. This helps the blades catch everything.
Blend on high for 45–60 seconds until completely smooth. Scrape down the sides if needed and blend again for 10 seconds.
Taste and adjust. Too thick? Add a splash of almond milk. Want it sweeter? Half a medjool date works perfectly.
Pour immediately and drink within 10 minutes for best texture and nutrition.

title: Chicken Quinoa Bowl
blurb: My go-to meal prep staple. Make a big batch Sunday, eat it for three days. Protein-packed, filling, and actually delicious cold.
benefits: Complete protein from both chicken and quinoa
Complex carbs for sustained energy
Easy to batch — scales perfectly for 4 servings

steps.text: Cook quinoa: rinse well, then combine with 160ml water and a pinch of salt. Bring to boil, cover and simmer for 12 minutes. Fluff with a fork and let cool.
Season chicken with salt, pepper and a drizzle of olive oil. Cook in a non-stick pan on medium-high for 6 minutes per side until cooked through. Rest 5 minutes before slicing.
Halve the cherry tomatoes, dice the cucumber. Toss with lemon juice, olive oil, salt and pepper.
Assemble: quinoa base, sliced chicken, vegetables. Finish with fresh herbs if using. Works warm or cold.

title: Berry Overnight Oats
tags: No Cook · Meal Prep · Gut Health
blurb: Sunday night, 8 minutes. Monday morning, breakfast is done. This is one of the highest-traffic recipes on my TikTok for good reason.
benefits: Beta-glucan fibre from oats feeds beneficial gut bacteria
Berries add antioxidants with no added sugar
Zero effort in the morning — it's already made

steps.text: Combine oats, almond milk, yoghurt, chia seeds, honey and vanilla in a jar or container. Stir well — make sure the chia seeds are evenly distributed.
Add the frozen berries on top (they thaw overnight and create a natural jam layer).
Cover and refrigerate for at least 6 hours, ideally overnight.
In the morning, stir, add fresh toppings if you like (granola, more fruit, nut butter) and eat cold.

title: Creamy Avocado Pasta
blurb: Creamy without cream. Ready in 20 minutes. The sauce is just blended avocado — which sounds weird until you try it.
benefits: Healthy monounsaturated fats from avocado
Dairy-free naturally — no substitutions needed
Ripe avocado creates genuine creaminess

steps.text: Cook pasta in well-salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve ½ cup of pasta water before draining.
While pasta cooks, blend avocado, garlic, lemon juice, olive oil, basil, salt and pepper until completely smooth.
Toss hot drained pasta with the avocado sauce. Add pasta water a splash at a time until you reach a silky consistency.
Serve immediately topped with cherry tomatoes and extra black pepper. This doesn't keep — eat it fresh.

title: No-Bake Energy Balls
blurb: 30 minutes of effort on Sunday gives you snacks for the whole week. High in fibre, naturally sweet, no refined sugar.
benefits: Dates provide natural sweetness without blood sugar spikes
Oats and seeds give slow-release energy
Batch-friendly — store in fridge up to 2 weeks

steps.text: Soak dates in warm water for 10 minutes if they feel dry. Drain well.
Blend dates in a food processor until they form a paste. Add oats, almond butter, cacao, chia seeds and salt. Pulse until it comes together as a sticky dough — don't over-blend, some texture is good.
Refrigerate the mixture for 15 minutes — easier to roll when cold.
Roll into balls (about 2 tbsp each) and roll in desiccated coconut. Store in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

title: Gut Health Smoothie Bowl
blurb: My most-requested recipe after the green smoothie. The thick consistency comes from the ratio — less liquid, more frozen fruit.
benefits: Live cultures from kefir or yoghurt support gut microbiome
Prebiotic fibre from banana and oats feeds good bacteria
High in antioxidants from mixed berries

steps.text: Blend frozen mango, banana, kefir and spinach with minimal liquid. The goal is a thick, spoonable consistency — stop as soon as it blends.
Pour into a cold bowl (chilling the bowl first keeps it thick longer).
Top with oats, fresh berries, a drizzle of honey and any other toppings you like — granola, coconut flakes, seeds.
Eat immediately before it melts.

title: Lemon Baked Salmon
blurb: The recipe I send to anyone who says they don't know how to cook fish. 4 ingredients, one pan, done in under 20 minutes.
benefits: Rich in omega-3 fatty acids for anti-inflammatory support
High-quality protein — around 35g per portion
Zero prep except seasoning

steps.text: Preheat oven to 200°C. Line a baking tray with parchment paper.
Pat the salmon dry. Drizzle with olive oil, season with garlic powder, salt and pepper.
Lay lemon slices underneath and on top of the fillet. Bake for 12–14 minutes depending on thickness. It's done when it flakes easily with a fork.
Rest for 2 minutes before serving. Finish with fresh dill if using.

title: High-Protein Chia Pudding
tags: No Cook · High Protein · Gut Health
blurb: Make it in a jar on Sunday night. It's dessert that works as breakfast. The protein version uses Greek yoghurt instead of just milk.
benefits: Chia seeds expand to create a gel that keeps you full
Greek yoghurt adds 15g protein per serving
Prebiotic fibre supports digestive health

steps.text: Whisk together chia seeds, yoghurt, almond milk, vanilla and honey in a jar.
Stir again after 5 minutes to break up any clumps that form.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight.
Top with fruit, granola, or a drizzle of nut butter before eating.
